title: Cathie Wood’s 2026 Outlook: The US Economy Is A Coiled Spring
gist: ARK's 2026 outlook argues the US economy is a 'coiled spring' primed to snap back after a multi-year rolling recession, driven by deregulation, tax cuts, falling inflation, and an AI/robotics/energy-storage/blockchain/multiomics productivity boom, with contrarian calls for dollar strength, P/E compression amid a continuing bull market, and gold at a historic valuation extreme versus a structurally scarcer bitcoin.

insights.claim: Despite three years of positive real GDP growth, the US economy has actually endured a 'rolling recession' -- sequential downturns in housing, manufacturing, non-AI capital spending, and low-to-middle income consumption -- that ARK frames as a coiled spring poised to snap back.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Sets up the bull case for a sharp 2026 acceleration in growth rather than a continuation of stagnation.

insights.claim: The Fed funds rate rose 22-fold, from 0.25% in March 2022 to 5.5% by July 2023 over sixteen months, and this rate shock is identified as the trigger that pushed housing, manufacturing, non-AI capex, and lower-income consumers into recession.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Implies anticipated Fed rate cuts in 2026 could unwind this drag symmetrically, powering a rebound in the same sectors.

insights.claim: Existing home sales fell 40% from 5.9 million annualized in January 2021 to 3.5 million in October 2023 -- a level last seen in November 2010 and matching rates last seen in the early 1980s despite the US population being about 35% larger today, indicating extreme pent-up housing demand.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: A larger population chasing the same unit-sales volume implies outsized latent demand that could fuel a housing volume rebound once rates fall.

insights.claim: Non-defense capital goods spending excluding aircraft peaked in mid-2022 and only just returned to that level, following a roughly 20-year plateau around $70 billion after the dot-com bust; ARK argues this former 'cap' is becoming a floor now that AI, robotics, energy storage, blockchain, and multiomics platforms are commercially ready, and explicitly states an AI bubble is 'years away.'
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Directly rebuts the AI-bubble narrative and frames current AI capex as the start of a much longer structural cycle rather than a peak.
insights.quote: In our view, an AI bubble is years away!

insights.claim: University of Michigan consumer sentiment among low- and middle-income earners has fallen to levels not seen since the early 1980s, and high-income sentiment has recently capitulated too, making consumer sentiment one of the most tightly coiled springs ARK expects to rebound.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: A sentiment reversal alongside falling inflation and tax refunds could be an early tell for a consumer-driven growth acceleration.

insights.claim: Deregulation across every economic sector is being driven by David Sacks, described as the first AI and Crypto 'Czar,' with the AI and digital-assets space particularly benefiting.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Signals a policy-driven tailwind specifically favoring AI and crypto-adjacent businesses.

insights.claim: Lower taxes on tips, overtime, and social security are expected to hand consumers a large refund this quarter, potentially lifting real disposable income growth from roughly 2% annualized in H2 2025 to about 8.3% this quarter.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: A jump of this magnitude would be a powerful, near-term catalyst for consumer spending data.

insights.claim: New 100% first-year depreciation on manufacturing facilities (for construction started before end of 2028), equipment, software, and domestic R&D -- made permanent and retroactive to January 1, 2025 -- is pushing the effective US corporate tax rate toward 10%, one of the lowest in the world.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: A near-10% effective tax rate plus full expensing is a major, durable capex incentive favoring domestic manufacturing and reshoring plays.

themes.theme: Inflation Rolling Over Faster Than Official Data Show

insights.claim: ARK expects CPI inflation to fall to a surprisingly low, if not negative, rate over the next few years.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: A negative-inflation call is a genuinely contrarian, high-conviction macro bet with major implications for Fed policy and bond duration.

insights.claim: WTI oil has dropped about 53% from its post-COVID peak of ~$124/barrel on March 8, 2022, and is down about 22% year-over-year, a direct disinflationary input to ARK's forecast.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Falling energy costs are a mechanical drag on headline CPI over the next several prints.

insights.claim: Existing single-family home price inflation has collapsed from about 24% year-over-year at its June 2021 peak to about 1.3%, while new single-family home prices are down about 15% since their October 2022 peak.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Shelter is the largest CPI component with a long lag, so this deceleration implies further downside to reported inflation ahead.

insights.claim: With new single-family home inventory near 500,000 -- a level last seen just before the 2007 Global Financial Crisis -- homebuilders cut prices year-over-year in Q4: Lennar -10%, KB Home -7%, D.R. Horton -3%, cuts ARK says will feed into CPI with a lag.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Builder price cuts are a leading indicator investors can track directly ahead of the lagged CPI shelter print.

insights.claim: Non-farm productivity rose 1.9% year-over-year against 3.2% growth in compensation per hour, producing only 1.2% unit labor cost inflation -- explicitly framed as showing no 1970s-style cost-push inflation.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Rules out a wage-price spiral as an inflation risk, supporting the case for continued Fed easing.
insights.quote: No Seventies-style cost-push-inflation in that number!

insights.claim: Truflation's alternative, high-frequency inflation measure shows inflation at 1.7% year-over-year, nearly 100 basis points below the official BLS CPI reading, suggesting official inflation data may be overstating true price pressure.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: If the alternative gauge is right, the Fed may be behind the curve on how much inflation has already fallen, supporting a faster rate-cut path.

insights.claim: ARK expects non-farm productivity growth to accelerate to 4-6% year-over-year over the next few years (versus 1.9% currently), driven by convergence among AI, robotics, energy storage, public blockchain, and multiomics technologies.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: This is the load-bearing assumption behind ARK's entire growth/inflation forecast -- worth tracking as the key falsifiable number.

insights.claim: ARK's framework holds that companies can deploy productivity gains only four ways -- margin expansion, increased R&D/investment, higher compensation, or lower prices -- and applies this to argue China could rebalance its economy by raising worker pay instead of over-investing.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Gives investors a lens for reading corporate earnings calls: which of the four levers a company pulls signals its competitive strategy.

insights.claim: China's investment has averaged about 40% of GDP since WTO entry in 2001 -- nearly double the US rate -- and ARK ties higher Chinese worker compensation to President Xi Jinping's 'anti-involution' goal of shifting the economy toward consumption and away from commoditized over-investment.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: A genuine shift toward Chinese consumption would ease global overcapacity/deflation exported via Chinese goods -- a theme to watch in China policy statements.

insights.claim: In the short term, technology-driven productivity gains are expected to keep slowing US employment growth, pushing unemployment from 4.4% toward 5.0%+ and pressuring the Fed to keep cutting rates, before deregulation and fiscal stimulus reaccelerate GDP growth in H2 2026.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Gives a specific unemployment threshold (5.0%+) and timeline (H2 2026 reacceleration) investors can use to check the thesis against incoming jobs data.

insights.claim: AI model training costs are falling about 75% per year and AI inference costs are dropping as much as 99% per year according to some benchmarks, a deflationary force ARK ties directly to productivity gains and disinflation.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: This cost curve is the mechanical engine behind ARK's productivity and disinflation forecasts -- a number to sanity-check against vendor pricing trends.

insights.claim: ARK forecasts nominal US GDP growth of 6-8% over the next few years, built from 5-7% productivity growth, 1% labor force growth, and inflation ranging from -2% to +1%.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: An explicit, decomposable macro forecast that can be checked quarter by quarter against BEA/BLS releases.

insights.claim: ARK likens the current AI/robotics/energy-storage/blockchain/multiomics convergence to the technology revolution ending in 1929 (internal combustion engine, electricity, telephony), during which short rates tracked nominal GDP while long rates fell on deflationary undercurrents, inverting the yield curve by about 100bps on average -- implying today's yield-curve inversions may signal a deflationary boom rather than a recession.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Reframes yield-curve inversion as a potential positive-productivity signal rather than a recession warning, changing how investors should read that indicator.

themes.theme: Gold's Historic Extreme vs Bitcoin's Metered Scarcity

insights.claim: In 2025 the gold price rose 65% while bitcoin fell 6%, a notable divergence between the two 'hard money' assets.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Challenges the assumption that gold and bitcoin move together as inflation hedges.
insights.locator: Gold Price Appreciation vs Bitcoin Price Depreciation

insights.claim: Gold's 166% rise from $1,600 to $4,300 since October 2022 is more likely explained by global wealth creation (MSCI World Equity Index +93%) outpacing gold's ~1.8% annual supply growth than by inflation fears, per ARK's alternative interpretation.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: If wealth creation rather than inflation fear is driving gold, that changes what would make gold reverse -- a growth slowdown, not falling inflation.
insights.locator: Gold Price Appreciation vs Bitcoin Price Depreciation

insights.claim: Bitcoin's price rose 360% over the same period against only ~1.3% annualized supply growth, and its supply growth is mathematically set to decelerate from ~0.82%/year to ~0.41%/year within two years; unlike gold miners, bitcoin miners cannot ramp production in response to higher prices.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Bitcoin's fixed, decelerating supply schedule is structurally more scarce than gold's price-responsive supply -- a key differentiator for long-term store-of-value positioning.
insights.locator: Gold Price Appreciation vs Bitcoin Price Depreciation

insights.claim: The ratio of gold's market cap to M2 money supply has been higher only once in 125 years -- during the Great Depression, when gold was fixed at $20.67/oz and M2 collapsed ~30% -- and has now surpassed even its 1980 peak, indicating gold is at a historically extreme valuation.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: An extreme, rarely-seen valuation ratio is a warning flag for gold bulls and a potential mean-reversion setup.

insights.claim: Following the two prior secular peaks in the Gold/M2 ratio (1934 and 1980), the DJIA returned 670% and 1,015% (6% and 12% annualized) over the subsequent 35 and 21 years respectively, with small caps averaging 12-13% annualized -- a pattern ARK cites as bullish for equities once gold/M2 mean-reverts.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Implies rotating out of gold into equities, especially small caps, near Gold/M2 extremes has historically been highly rewarding.

insights.claim: Bitcoin's return correlation to gold, commodities, the S&P 500, bonds, and REITs (0.14-0.28) is lower than the correlation between the S&P 500 and bonds (0.27) using weekly returns since 2020, making bitcoin a strong diversification asset for allocators seeking higher risk-adjusted returns.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Quantifies bitcoin's diversification benefit versus a traditional stock-bond correlation, supporting a strategic allocation case.
insights.locator: Gold Price Appreciation vs Bitcoin Price Depreciation

themes.theme: A Contrarian Call for Dollar Strength

insights.claim: Against the popular 'end of US exceptionalism' narrative -- the dollar (DXY) fell 11% in H1 2025 (the steepest H1 drop since 1973) and 9% for the full year (the biggest calendar-year drop since 2017) -- ARK expects the dollar to strengthen as US fiscal policy, monetary policy, deregulation, and tech breakthroughs raise US return on invested capital relative to the rest of the world, drawing a direct parallel to early Reaganomics in the 1980s when the dollar nearly doubled.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: A dollar-strength call is directly tradeable against consensus positioning and has knock-on effects for commodities, EM assets, and multinational earnings.

themes.theme: AI Capex Supercycle and the Race to Monetize

insights.claim: US data center systems investment (compute, networking, storage) grew 47% in 2025 to nearly $500 billion and is projected to grow another 20% in 2026 to about $600 billion, far above the $150-200 billion/year trend of the ten years before ChatGPT's launch.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Quantifies the scale of the AI capex supercycle, a number to track against actual hyperscaler capex guidance for signs of overbuild or shortfall.

insights.claim: ARK argues the biggest returns on AI capex may accrue not to public semiconductor and cloud companies but to AI-native companies still private and not yet in public equity markets.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Implies public AI infrastructure stocks may not capture the full value creation, favoring private-market/pre-IPO exposure.

insights.claim: Consumers are adopting AI at twice the pace at which they adopted the internet in the 1990s, per ARK's research.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Supports a faster monetization timeline for consumer AI products than the internet build-out provided.

insights.claim: By year-end 2025, OpenAI's annualized run-rate revenue reportedly reached about $20 billion (up 12.5x from $1.6 billion a year earlier) and Anthropic's reached about $9 billion (up 90x from $100 million), with both reportedly weighing IPOs within one to two years.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: The revenue growth rates and IPO timelines are concrete markers investors can watch for the next major tech listings.

insights.claim: OpenAI's CEO of Applications, Fidji Simo, is quoted framing 2026 as the year AI leadership will go to whichever company turns frontier research into products that are genuinely useful, illustrated by the new ChatGPT Health feature.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Signals the competitive axis in AI is shifting from raw model capability to product/UX execution, relevant for picking AI application winners.
insights.quote: AI models are capable of far more than how most people experience them day to day, and 2026 is about closing that gap.

insights.claim: ARK argues enterprises that fail to train AI models on their own proprietary data and iterate quickly in 2026 risk being left behind by more aggressive competitors, as many enterprise AI deployments remain stalled by bureaucracy, inertia, and unbuilt data foundations.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Frames enterprise data-readiness as the key differentiator for which companies capture AI productivity gains versus which get disrupted.

themes.theme: Valuation Can Compress Even in a Bull Market

insights.claim: ARK expects equity market P/E multiples to fall back toward their 20x average of the last 35 years even as it remains bullish, citing precedent: the S&P 500 returned 21% annualized from mid-1993 to mid-1997 as its P/E fell from 36x to 10x, and 14% annualized from 2002 to 2007 as P/E compressed from 21x to 17x.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Reframes 'expensive market' fears: multiple compression need not derail the bull case if earnings/GDP growth outpaces price appreciation.
